ValidationRule.description: When users change Type, we require them to choose a reason why they changed it
ValidationRule.errorConditionFormula: /*Not AI User, and New.Type !=Prior.Type and Prior.Type!=null. Excluded integartion and edit admin and include check for sub type asked by Naresh AND( TEXT(Reason_For_Type_SubType_Change__c)=null ,TEXT(Type)!=PRIORVALUE(Type) ,PRIORVALUE(Type)!=null ,NOT(CONTAINS($User.Username,'ai.user@')) ,NOT(CONTAINS($User.Username,'integration@')) ,NOT(CONTAINS($User.Username,'sfdcedit@')) ,TEXT(Owned_by_Company__c)='LSDX' ) */ AND( OR( AND( ISCHANGED(Type) , NOT( ISBLANK( PRIORVALUE(Type))) ) , AND( ISCHANGED(Sub_Type__c) , NOT( ISBLANK( PRIORVALUE(Sub_Type__c))) ) ) , NOT($Permission.C_PERM_2400_Bypass_Case_V_RULES) , TEXT( Owned_by_Company__c)='LSDX' , fxRecordTypeDevName__c='Customer_Care_General'/*Asked by Isa on Email scope Of AI is only for CC general cases with case origin : Email*/ , TEXT( Origin)='Email' , ISBLANK( TEXT( Reason_For_Type_SubType_Change__c)) )
ValidationRule.errorMessage: V.RULE_CASE_053: Enter a reason for changing the type- or subtype.
